rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed method is conceptually positioned clearly.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally involves checking whether the theoretical argument from assumptions to convergence claim is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical speedup and tolerance-matching claims, so judging whether reported evidence supports those claims is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact includes a first-of-its-kind novelty claim, making assessment of contribution delta a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended judgment must preserve uncertainty about what depth of review was intended.
rationale.RX.2: The broad request requires avoiding stronger conclusions than the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection of responsibilities and not perform the requested assessment.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harm from overstating solver guarantees or empirical validity must be treated as active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
